observation_summary: The ListPopulation iterator allowed modification of the underlying chromosome list, violating the expected encapsulation. The test expected an UnsupportedOperationException when calling iterator().remove(), but the operation succeeded because the iterator was not wrapped.
hypothesis: The iterator() method was returning the raw iterator of the internal 'chromosomes' list, which allows external modification. The fix should ensure the iterator is returned from an unmodifiable view of the list.
prediction: The code will change from returning 'chromosomes.iterator()' to returning 'getChromosomes().iterator()', where getChromosomes() returns an unmodifiable view.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows the change from 'chromosomes.iterator()' to 'getChromosomes().iterator()'. This confirms that the method was using the wrong internal data access strategy, failing to enforce the required immutability contract.
reasoning_summary: The defect is an incorrect implementation of the iterator() method. It failed to protect the internal state of the ListPopulation object. This is a procedural/method-level logic error regarding how the iterator is exposed, fitting the Algorithm/Method category as it corrects the internal data access strategy.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While it involves an interface (Iterator), the root cause is the internal implementation of the iterator() method failing to provide an unmodifiable view, which is a procedural logic error rather than a signature mismatch.
This is a local procedural fix within an existing method, not a design-level capability correction.
